architecture_decision.rationale: Q1=A keeps TelemetryActor.on_event(str,dict) contract stable (14 existing telemetry tests untouched) and telemetry_actor.py NT-import-free (base install testable); no red-line reason to subclass NT Actor. Q2=A: normalizer produces money-safe dict via type(event).to_dict(event), keys qty/price/pnl validated by existing money gate; dataclass adds no wire-boundary safety. Q3=deploy-per-spec: the NT MessageBus only exists after node.build() inside deploy, so a global singleton cannot subscribe to a per-node bus; per-spec TelemetryActor gives natural session_id isolation. All three are the handoff-recommended defaults; no escalation needed.

foundation_scan_round_4_findings.msgbus: nautilus_trader.common.component.MessageBus.subscribe(topic, handler) supports */? wildcards (empirically dispatches events.order.* to a events.order.ST-1 publish); node.kernel.msgbus is the subscribe path; node.trader.add_actor exists
foundation_scan_round_4_findings.publish_topics: order events -> events.order.{strategy_id}; position -> events.position.{strategy_id}; fills -> events.fills.{instrument_id} (execution/engine.pyx:910/918/926)
foundation_scan_round_4_findings.to_dict: static form type(event).to_dict(event) (instance .to_dict() raises); money fields str, enums via *_to_str ('BUY' not str(OrderSide.BUY)=='1')
foundation_scan_round_4_findings.handler_invocation: NT calls handlers synchronously (component.pyx:2834 sub.handler(msg), no await) — an async handler subscribed directly drops its coroutine

test_run_summary.gates: make verify (base: 156 pass / 7 skip = NT-gated tests) + make verify/make test-nt (real nautilus-trader 1.230: 205 pass / 0 skip); ruff fmt+lint clean
test_run_summary.note: test_wire_shapes.py excluded from baseline (Plan 01 DEV-01-WIRE-FIXTURES), not introduced by this plan.

failure_mode_contract.note: All contract-table test functions grep-verified present (lesson #25, 6/6 + 1 extra forward-crash guard). Contract table file/trigger locations corrected to the Q1=A duck-typed bridge implementation (original table assumed Q1=B Actor subclass).

deviations.reason: Q1=A duck-typed bridge / Q2=A dict payload / Q3=deploy-per-spec — handoff §3 recommended defaults, executor decision, no red-line conflict.

DOUBLY DEAD (either root cause alone kills the reject bridge). Root cause A — literal topic mismatch: subscribed events.order.OrderDenied but NT publishes every order event on events.order.{strategy_id} (execution/engine.pyx:910 + :1341-1343), never matches. Root cause B — async handler on sync bus: NT invokes handlers synchronously (common/component.pyx:2834 sub.handler(msg), no await), so the async on_order_denied subscribed directly returned a dropped coroutine; even a fixed topic would never publish. Fixed: subscribe events.order.* + a sync dispatcher that filters via a CONCRETE type discriminator (type(event).__name__ == 'OrderDenied', string fallback for optional-NT-import) and schedules the async publish (run_coroutine_threadsafe off-loop / ensure_future on-loop, failure logged pre_trade_reject_publish_failed). safety-validator block-level constraint: 4 order event classes share `reason` (OrderDenied/OrderRejected/OrderModifyRejected/OrderCancelRejected) so hasattr/attribute-set/duck-typed discrimination is FORBIDDEN (would misclassify OrderRejected as a pre-trade denial); the hasattr in on_order_denied is a post-type-match shape guard, not a classifier. Wire contract unchanged; real-NT OrderDenied test added (lesson #25). The pre-existing test_bootstrap_subscribes_when_bus_present asserted subscribe-was-called but not the topic (lesson #17 happy-path gap) — strengthened to assert events.order.*.

Real NT OrderDenied carries no side/quantity/price/rule_id/reference_price/ts_seconds (骨架 getattr-ed them). Kept ducktyped getattr for graceful degradation (real events → empty, fake tests → present); derive ts from real ts_event(ns); added a shape guard (pre_trade_reject_event_shape_mismatch) skipping drifted events missing reason/instrument_id. 5-field wire contract unchanged; fingerprint degrades to a (symbol, ts) correlation handle (docstring already declares it non-tamper-evidence).

plan Task 1 referenced tests/test_telemetry_envelope.py as existing; it does not. envelope contract is covered by test_nats_envelope.py + test_telemetry_money_contract.py; payload/bridge contracts landed in new tests/test_telemetry_nt_bridge.py. test_telemetry_actor.py / _failure_modes.py unchanged (Q1=A keeps them valid).

Orchestration incident (fixed immediately, zero artifact loss): tdd-enforcer-00b used `git stash` + `git checkout 232c5a6 -- .` to compare a historical version — the checkout is NOT read-only and overwrote the working tree (stashed the executor's uncommitted plan deviation-log edits, reverted src/arx_runner/telemetry_actor.py to the old version). tdd-enforcer recovered (git checkout HEAD -- telemetry_actor.py + git stash pop) and grep-verified all deviation sections intact; executor re-verified at close-out (git show HEAD:<plan> grep 4 deviation sections present + telemetry_actor 4 symbols present + working tree clean). Same root cause as ecosystem lesson #29 (an operation assumed read-only has a write side effect). Prevention: historical-version queries use `git show <ref>:<path>` (stdout only) or `git diff <ref1> <ref2> -- <path>`, never `git checkout <ref> -- .`. CEO decision: extend lesson #29 (git-history-query sub-clause) rather than mint a new C-number; tdd-enforcer co-authors the extension text, executor acknowledges here.

lesson_29_extension_ack.extended: ecosystem historical-lessons.md #29 gets a 'git history query' sub-clause; custos .claude/rules/historical-lessons.md #29 may add an alias
lesson_29_extension_ack.root_cause: git checkout <ref> -- . / -- <path> is not read-only — it overwrites the working tree
lesson_29_extension_ack.prevention: use git show <ref>:<path> (stdout) or git diff <ref1> <ref2> -- <path> for historical comparison; never git checkout <ref> -- <path> as a read op
lesson_29_extension_ack.race_window_recheck: executor re-verified committed plan (4 deviation sections) + telemetry_actor.py (4 symbols) intact + working tree clean; no stash-pop loss
lesson_29_extension_ack.co_author: tdd-enforcer-00b (incident victim + beneficiary) drafts the lesson text; executor acknowledges

red_lines_check.grep_evidence: log.*api_key none in src/tests; external venue client outside nautilus_host.py none; stop_all_strategies/force_shutdown in reconcile.py none; float( in money path (touched files) none; telemetry payload only curated summary fields (symbol/side/qty/price/pnl/fee/ts), money via to_dict str + Money split value/ccy; attach + publish failures log-and-continue, never crash NT thread (红线 0.3)

self_reflect.round_1: found + fixed: the risk bridge sync dispatcher and host _on_node_task_done scheduled coroutines with ensure_future but held no reference — Python can GC a pending task mid-flight, silently dropping a rejection publish (对账不静默) or leaking the actor's loops. Track pending futures in a set, discard on done (commit e688242).
self_reflect.round_2: clean — 4 red-line greps pass; source diff reviewed, no logic/boundary/error-handling issues; noted heartbeat-redundancy + fingerprint-weakening as documented follow-ups (F1/F2 in close-out), not blockers.

follow_ups: F1: per-deploy TelemetryActor heartbeats coexist with the __main__ runner fallback heartbeat loop on the same subject (multiple session_ids). Harmless in v1 (consumer dedups by session); consolidation is a follow-up.

F2: OrderDenied fingerprint is (symbol, ts)-based since NT's event carries no side/qty/price; a stronger fingerprint would need an NT order-cache lookup by client_order_id (wire-contract change, mid-risk).
F3: _attach_observability uses tenant_id/runner_id or '' defensively; CLI always supplies them, empty tokens fail-fast at build_subject on publish.
